mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and stable inflation (CPI) signals a balanced expansion phase where corporate margins are preserved, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1.5 million units indicates strong consumer confidence and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns during housing boom regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when HOUST exceeds 1500 units.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index above 102.5 signals increased manufacturing activity, which correlates with higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns when INDPRO exceeds 102.5.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer credit availability (RSAFS) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates abundant liquidity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150,000 jobs indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate revenue, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns when PAYEMS exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate (≤5.33%) consistently predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to reduced discount rates and increased risk appetite.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is ≤5.33%.
